text: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias: if an AI is trained on unfair data, it can make biased decisions, for example in hiring or loan approvals. This directly affects fairness, as such systems may treat people unequally. Privacy is another key issue, since AI often uses vast amounts of personal data. We must ask who controls this data and how it is protected. Transparency is also crucial: people need to understand how AI reaches its conclusions. To address these challenges, responsible AI development emphasises clear guidelines, regular testing and open discussion. Only by considering these ethical dimensions can we ensure technology serves everyone.
